title: Day 6 · Session JSONL + Three-Layer Memory
task: Use the echo tool to say hello

frames.caption: The CLI sees no --resume or -c flag, so it creates a new Session. The prompt enters messages and is also written as the first JSONL row.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hello

frames.caption: AnthropicProvider calls the model with messages, tools, and the system prompt. The system path can now include AGENT.md and the MEMORY.md index, but it never becomes part of the messages list.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hello

frames.caption: Day 6's gate is the context boundary: resume restores Session.history into messages, execution only appends JSONL rows, and build_system_prompt(cwd) layers core instructions, AGENT.md, and MEMORY.md.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hello

frames.caption: echo is a readonly tool, so the permission engine allows it. After the tool returns, the harness appends both the assistant tool_use and user tool_result to JSONL.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hello

frames.caption: The same conversation now exists in two forms: the in-memory messages list for the next model call, and the disk ledger used by --resume and -c.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hello

card.label: final: I used echo to say "hello".

frames.caption: On the second model request, the model reads the tool_result and returns a final answer. Before returning, the Agent Loop appends assistant(final) to the session JSONL file.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hello

frames.caption: The terminal prints the session id. Next time, uv run agent-code -c reads the latest JSONL file, restores the messages list, and continues the same conversation.

messages.content: Use the echo tool to say hello
